ratings.comment: Paris lectures are very boring, but filled with information so if you pay attention you will do well in the class. He also records lectures which I really appreciated. Projects and exams make up the grade. Grading was quite generous and transparent. Final project B+ tree and final exam was a nightmare but everything else until then was easy
Nice professor, explains everything in lectures. Don't fall behind. B+ tree was a nightmare.

Paris is mediocre at best. Generic CS professor here at UW, nothing special at all. Take the course with him if you need the credit and absolutely have to. Otherwise theres absolutely no reason to go out of your way to take the class with him. Gave him a 2 instead of 1 because he records his lectures.

I think the projects were a bit disorganized at times, but I also think Paris is a great guy and genuinely cares about his students. CS 564 will be hard if you don't show up to class, so make sure you go.
Awesome professor! lectures are clear and concise. If you pay attention in his class you will understand everything and an A grade is guaranteed. He records lectures, and that comes in handy.

I thought Professor was really willing to help out his students outside of lecture time and was open to questions. His lecture may have been a bit dry, but overall the projects and exams were very fair. Seems like he truly cares about the success of his students.
Listening to his dry lectures is nothing better than reading the textbook... Exams and projects push you to study, but overall the class was not enjoyable at all

Extremely rude to students. Projects are stolen from other schools and have little to no guidance. Blamed student for cheating on the final without having any evidence and dropped their grades from A's to B/C's or lower. He answers questions poorly and clearly does not care about his students. The class isn't terribly hard but be aware.
terrible professor. reads off of slides and just replies to every question with 'good question' and chooses not to answer them. Would not recommend

Every time I got a chance to talk with Prof Koutris he was very courteous and confident I would succeed in his course, even when I was not. His lectures have some valuable material, and he's really concerned about his students' success in understanding the work they do.
